mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Steps
mnemonics.content: B-A-F-D-C-I-R: Baseline, Analyze, Forecast, Develop plan, Implement, Review (forms the CAPACITY cycle)

mnemonics.title: Scaling Types
mnemonics.content: VU: Vertical = Up (add to existing), HO: Horizontal = Out (add more nodes)

mnemonics.title: Key Metrics
mnemonics.content: CPU, MEM, DISK, NET (think of them as the 'core' resources)

acronyms.usage: Defines performance guarantees between IT and business
Standards for network and system service performance
Maximum acceptable downtime for IT services
Maximum acceptable data loss measured in time
Metrics to measure capacity planning success
Repository of IT asset and configuration information

visualTips.visualization: Picture a circular flow: Baseline → Analyze → Forecast → Plan → Implement → Monitor → Review → Repeat (continuous improvement loop)
Imagine a gauge with green zone (70-85% optimal), yellow zone (85-95% warning), red zone (95-100% critical)
Vertical = stacking blocks higher on one tower. Horizontal = building more towers side by side to distribute weight.
Balance scale: over-provisioning tips scale toward wasted money (cost), under-provisioning tips toward service failure (risk). Goal: balanced center.

analogies.analogy: Like planning restaurant seating - need enough tables for expected customers, not too many (wasted space) or too few (angry customers leaving)
Similar to weather forecasting - use historical patterns, current conditions, and seasonal factors to predict future needs
Like taking your car's baseline mileage - know normal performance to detect when something is wrong
Like adding more checkout counters during holiday shopping instead of making one super-large counter
Like a warranty or guarantee - promise of performance level that must be met, with consequences if not delivered
